page: Microsoft Azure DC128eds_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the DCEDV6 server family. Powered by an Intel processor with an x86_64 architecture, this instance features 128 dedicated virtual CPUs (vCPUs) and 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding a ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, ensuring dedicated compute resource allocation.

This server type includes a substantial local storage capacity of 7559 GB and is designated as Premium Storage capable, allowing for high-performance disk configurations. It does not include bundled GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses, focusing its hardware profile entirely on high-capacity compute, memory, and local storage density.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the DC128eds_v6 offers a dense consolidation of dedicated Intel vCPUs and massive local storage, making it suitable for enterprise workloads that require high resource density without the overhead of discrete GPU hardware. The combination of 128 dedicated vCPUs, 512.0 GB of RAM, and over 7.5 TB of local storage makes this instance well-suited for large-scale databases, memory-intensive enterprise applications, and data warehousing workloads.
description: Microsoft Azure DC128eds_v6 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the DCEDV6 family, built on an x86_64 Intel processor architecture and managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. This instance provides 128 dedicated vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM, establishing a resource ratio of 4.0 GB of memory per core. A key feature of this configuration is its integrated 7559 GB of local storage, alongside compatibility with Premium Storage. It does not feature GPU accelerators or complimentary public IPv4 addresses. Designed for high-density enterprise deployments, the DC128eds_v6 is structured for large-scale database management, memory-intensive applications, and data warehousing operations where substantial local storage and dedicated compute resources are required.
